food_subgroup: Flavoured tea drinks with cream foam

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for peach-flavoured oolong macchiato with cream foam. FOODON_03311737 (oolong tea beverage) is the closest parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0EJW (Tea, oolong) is the closest parent. No specific code exists for flavoured oolong macchiato beverages with cream foam topping.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 227613007 represents oolong tea as a substance. The full beverage with peach flavouring and macchiato cream foam has no exact SNOMED CT code.

preparation_method: blended with peach syrup or flavouring
topped with salted cream foam or macchiato foam

serving.natural_language_portion: One medium-sized cup of White Peach Oolong Macchiato as served at The Alley Singapore, approximately 500 ml, including oolong tea base, peach flavouring, and cream foam topping.

nutrient_source.title: Oolong tea, brewed; and cream/milk foam component estimated from USDA FoodData Central dairy cream and sugar entries; peach syrup contribution estimated from USDA SR Legacy sucrose-based syrup data
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central (Foundation Foods / SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for White Peach Oolong Macchiato as a composite beverage. Nutrient values are estimated by component: (1) oolong tea brewed base (USDA FoodData Central FDC ID 171917, Beverages, tea, oolong, brewed), (2) cream foam layer estimated from USDA heavy whipping cream and whole milk data, (3) white peach syrup contribution estimated from USDA sucrose syrup and peach nectar data. A medium 500 ml serving is assumed to contain approximately 350 ml oolong tea, 80 ml cream foam, and 70 ml peach syrup. Per-100g values are derived from this composite estimate.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021 — sucrose and sugar-sweetened beverages reference; oolong tea GI analogous estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for White Peach Oolong Macchiato. GI of 55 is estimated as a medium-GI value based on: (1) plain oolong tea has negligible GI contribution; (2) the dominant glycemic driver is the white peach syrup (sucrose-based), which has a GI of approximately 65 per University of Sydney GI Database; (3) the cream foam component (fat and protein) lowers the blended GI; (4) a composite GI of 55 (medium) is defensible for this sugar-sweetened tea beverage with dairy foam. Glycemic load per 500 ml serving = (55 x 42 g carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = 23.1.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content; moderate sugar load
hypertension.guidance: With approximately 90 mg sodium per 500 ml serving, this beverage poses minimal direct sodium risk for hypertensive individuals, though the added sugar content and caloric load should be considered in the context of overall dietary pattern and weight management.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High added sugar from peach syrup; medium glycemic index; significant glycemic load per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: With an estimated glycemic load of 23.1 per medium serving and approximately 42 g of carbohydrate predominantly from added sucrose-based peach syrup, this beverage is not recommended for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes without portion control or sugar-free substitution; regular consumption may impair glycaemic control.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from cream foam; high added sugar contributing to dyslipidaemia risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The cream foam topping contributes saturated fat (approximately 8.5 g per 500 ml serving), and the high added sugar load may adversely affect triglyceride levels; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption and consider requesting reduced-sugar or no-foam variants.

llm_training.natural_language_description: White Peach Oolong Macchiato (Medium) is a Taiwanese-style flavoured tea beverage sold in Singapore by The Alley, a premium tea brand. It consists of a chilled oolong tea base infused or blended with white peach syrup or flavouring, topped with a layer of lightly salted or sweetened cream foam (macchiato-style). The drink is served cold in a medium cup of approximately 500 ml. The flavour profile is floral and fruity from the oolong tea and peach, with a creamy, slightly savoury contrast from the foam topping. The texture transitions from the smooth foam layer to the lighter tea base beneath. Nutritionally, the beverage is moderate in calories (approximately 310 kcal per 500 ml serving) and contains significant added sugar from the peach syrup, contributing to a medium glycemic index of approximately 55 and a glycemic load of 23.1 per serving. The cream foam adds saturated fat and a small amount of protein and calcium. This drink is not suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes without modification due to its high sugar content. It is vegetarian and halal-friendly but not vegan or dairy-free. Oolong tea polyphenols may offer antioxidant benefits, though these are diluted in the composite beverage. The drink is popular in Singapore's bubble tea and specialty tea segment and is representative of the Taiwanese milk tea culture adapted for Southeast Asian markets.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for this composite commercial beverage. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data for oolong tea, dairy cream, and sucrose-based syrup. The GI is an analogous estimate based on the University of Sydney GI Database for sucrose-sweetened beverages. Confidence is medium because the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible but not based on direct laboratory analysis of this specific product.
notes: Composite beverage nutrient values estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data (oolong tea FDC ID 171917, heavy cream, whole milk, sucrose syrup). Serving size assumed as 500 ml / 500 g for a medium cup. Per-100g values derived from composite ratio: approximately 70% oolong tea base, 16% cream foam, 14% peach syrup by volume. trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero per USDA dairy cream data; trace amounts below detection threshold reported as 0.0). dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 (naturally zero for a liquid beverage with no solid plant matter). vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reliably reported for this composite beverage in the source database. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reliably reported for this composite beverage in the source database. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reliably reported for this composite beverage in the source database. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reliably reported for this composite beverage in the source database. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reliably reported for this composite beverage in the source database. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reliably reported for this composite beverage in the source database. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reliably reported for this composite beverage in the source database. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reliably reported for this composite beverage in the source database. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reliably reported for this composite beverage in the source database. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reliably reported for this composite beverage in the source database. glycemic_index set to 55 (medium) as an analogous estimate; the dominant glycemic driver is the sucrose-based peach syrup (GI ~65 per University of Sydney GI Database), moderated by the cream foam fat and protein content. GI source: University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021. Cuisine tagged as Chinese per input; The Alley is a Taiwanese-origin brand operating in Singapore. Multilingual names provided for Singaporean context per G6 rules.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: dietary_fibre_g (0.0), sodium_mg (18.0), saturated_fat_g (1.7), and sugar_g (7.9) are all present as numeric values. dietary_fibre_g 0.0 is justified as naturally zero for a liquid beverage with no solid plant matter.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index is 55 (numeric, greater than zero), gi_category is medium, glycemic_load_per_serving is 23.1, gi_source references University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021. Analogous estimate basis is fully documented.
